review_title: Evernote, ever present across all my devices
description: I have been using Evernote for 5 years now. My overall experience have been very positive, I really like their environment and the multi-task approach covering notes, calendar, task reminder, etc.
author: Thaura G.
pros: Ease to use, multi-task, real-time edition, access easy across devices. Its all in one approach is great.
cons: Value for money, it is quite expensive compare to alternative tools. It is true that it’s multitask environment is very convenient, however, some users choose to navigate through multiple tools to avoid the costs.

review_title: A trustworthy center for coordinating outreach and content notes.
description: It’s helped standardize how we plan content and track outreach outreach efforts, so we can keep our messaging consistent across social, email and reports especially with a lean team all wearing a few hats.
author: Wesley M. B.
pros: Evernote Teams provides a centralized space to capture meeting notes, outreach drafts, donor communication templates, and campaign plans. I particularly love the tagging feature and shared notebooks even though we’re a small team it makes it a walk in the park to keep everything in sync, even when remote.
cons: The formatting can also come across as somewhat barebones compared to Notion or Google Docs. Also, syncing sometimes lags if more than one person is editing a note at the same time.

author: David S.
pros: It was solid, worked pretty well until their unbelievable manner of them deciding to raise their subscriptions and made it practically impossible to cancel.
cons: Zero stars. Evernote's Scrooge of software—Christmas Eve, mid-subscription, caps on exports, legacy links nuked, cancel button buried. Support? ‘Check settings.' Closed through January six. Bah Hum Bug.

review_title: A Powered AI perfect for team management with high sucess rate tool.
description: This product meets our excpectations and we simply overjoyed by its centralization features in the workplace, like sharing notes, documents and files becomes easy. This product is so amazing as it works across different platforms. This tool realy stands out.
author: Juan D.
pros: The Best reason why we liked this product is by using search command functionality. Even in depth, hidden files searches using this tool is a big plus. You should try it your self. Amazing...
cons: Offline sucess for this product is a thing to consider, but still it's a win win.

description: Evernote Teams is a solid tool for organizing and sharing information in a team setting. While its reliable and feature-rich, the cost and occasional syncing issues hold it back from being perfect
author: Umut Ö.
pros: The shared notebooks and team collab features are excellent. Its easy to organize, share, and search through notes across a team
cons: The pricing feels steep for what it offers compared to competitors. Also, the UI can feel a bit outdated, and syncing occasionally seems to have delays
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Last couple of year, i used evernote for my personal records. Easily i can take notes and make schedules in it. Sharing files with clients/team member is very secure. Due to cloud based operation, i can open files from anywhere. Its very helpful.
author: Mahmudul islam S.
pros: As a notes i keep using evernote. I can use multiple device to control my documents. Real time synchronization available for multiple devices. To keep my schedule, reminder options helps me a lot. Collaboration features is very unique and fast, i can share my documents with my team member easily.
cons: I did not have any complain about it. For free version i am happy, but sometime it ask us to upgrade.

description: Pretty good experience however, it did not match our standard, that's why we did not use the tool in the long term
author: Joseph T.
pros: It's a nice tool that helps to stay organized and helps teamwork. It also helps for project management
cons: The pricing is a bit high, also the ease of use was not very good coming from other products which makes it not easy to implement
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Managing Tasks and Software Applications with Evernote.
description: Overall, Evernote was perfect for my company to track, archive, and achieve everything we needed to do. Any small issue or complication was easily resolved by a great human resource center.
author: Brandon M.
pros: What I liked most about Evernote Teams is the ease it provided for myself to communicate with my group and my team, both together and individually. It provided a clear path to what was needed to be done, and gave me a path to complete my tasks without getting confused. It saved me a lot if time.
cons: Overall it was good, but I feel like it works better with large groups than it did if i wanted to use it privately.

description: I would highly recommend this platform for collaborating on projects with remote users.
author: Josh P.
pros: Easy collaboration and cloud sharing of notes, documents and more.
cons: New users, especially users coming from other platforms can struggle with the UI at first.

review_title: A simple and reliable digital notebook, but limited beyond note-taking
description: For straightforward note-taking, Evernote is dependable and practical. It’s best suited for users who want a simple, digital notebook, but those seeking project management or advanced productivity features may find it limited.
author: Yujin C.
pros: I’ve been using Evernote since 2018, primarily as a creative writing tool rather than a task manager. It works well for organizing ideas, drafting content, and capturing notes in one place. The biggest advantage is its simplicity. It’s easy to get started without needing tutorials. Synchronization across phone, laptop, and desktop is fast and reliable, and offline note-taking also works smoothly. I also appreciate that Evernote allows attaching PDFs, images, and audio files, which makes it feel more versatile than a paper notebook.
cons: While generally reliable, I have encountered occasional errors, such as double typing issues or content not saving properly when working on very long notes. These glitches were frustrating but happened mostly with extended drafts. Another limitation is that Evernote doesn’t go much further than being a digital notebook. Unlike newer productivity tools, it lacks advanced features for task management, collaboration, or flexible databases.

author: Kristiana B.
pros: Good functions and additional features for a notes app
cons: Too much different options, app was too busy
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: My experience with Evernote Teams has been great since the beginning. It has so many features for improving productivity within the page as it is used for team collaboration and project management. Even though I sometimes face small issues and bigginers may face difficulties when starting because it has a lots of features.
author: Abdul Malik Y.
pros: Evernote Teams has a seamless collaboration features that made me like it most. I found it easy when sharing notes, notebooks, and tasks with Evernote Teams, which significantly enhances productivity and ensures everyone is on the same platform. Also, the search works greatly, making it easy to find specific notes and documents quickly. It comprehensive integration with Google drive makes it easily accessible and efficient.
cons: The few challenges I have encountered with Evernote Teams, sometimes I found it challenging when it comes to refreshing the page. Again I think that the pricing is very costly for smaller teams, which might prevent people from using it. Addition, the user interface is somehow complicated to beginers as it has so many features that might require an introductory learning before getting to know the user interface.

description: Overall, it is fast and helps me in note-taking and documentation.
author: Sampad M.
pros: I like the drag and drop feature to organize notes and notebooks. It also provides co-editing in real-time and we can link notes to each others.
cons: The mobile editor is not as powerful as the web version. I did not have any other dislikes.

description: Best for: people who need a powerful note-taking and organization tool for tracking notes, property details, and meeting notes.
author: Zuen M.
pros: Cross-Device Syncing and organization ability
cons: Free Plan is Limited and Occasional Syncing Issues

description: great program with very little issues when setting up or using
author: Shauntel G.
pros: its so user friendly and the ease of use is great
cons: nothing at all! it is a great user friendly program
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Good but could be more stable
author: Nuno S.
pros: The search function is great, the syncing across devices also works well.
cons: The constant changes in UI have made it har to keep up.

description: It's a good app overall. I think it's a good one to have
author: Megan B.
pros: How simple it was to use and navigate within the app.
cons: I didn't really find anything I didn't care for

description: Evernote Teams is very good for note taking, to do lists and capturing things so you don't forget. Highly recommended.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I love that I can take notes on Evernote and also upload my PDFs on there. I also use it for my to do list and checking off items I have done. The search functionality is also really cool!
cons: I use the free version of Evernote, but it seems that there is a limit to the number of notes I can create, even after deleting some notes to make space.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Evernote Teams is a game-changer for collaborative productivity. Its seamless integration of note-taking, task management, and file sharing, coupled with robust security measures, makes it indispensable for teams. With cross-platform functionality, it's the ultimate solution for boosting productivity and streamlining workflow. Highly recommended.
cons: Evernote Teams disappoints with its cumbersome interface, frequent glitches, and limited customization options. Concerns over reliability in safeguarding sensitive data and a steep learning curve further dampen its appeal. Organizations may find more user-friendly and dependable collaborative tools elsewhere.

author: Mickael L.
pros: good over wiew of all tasks and easy to modify them and possibility to switch assignment in the team
cons: I needed more view possibilities that'a why i switch to notion for my personal projects, but still using it in my proffessionel projects.

author: Julien P.
pros: Efficient tool Capacity to work with a lof of people
cons: UX could be improve sometimes. The integration with other tools could be also improved.

description: Overall, Evernote Teams is a popular collaboration tool with a focus on note-taking and information sharing. I appreciate its Shared workspaces, task management, and real-time editing facilitate teamwork and project management, Powerful search, flexible organization, and offline access help manage information effectively.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Certain features allow simultaneous editing of notes, enabling a more dynamic and efficient teamwork experience. Notes and files can be accessed even without internet connection, ensuring work can continue seamlessly even while offline.
cons: Evernote Teams' free plan offers limited features and storage, potentially making it unsuitable for larger teams or those with extensive collaboration needs. Upgrading to paid plans can incur significant costs depending on team size and required storage space.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Shreyash M.
pros: Made workflow simple and organized and helped in tracking developments within the team.
cons: The price for professional version is pretty high for a small or mid-size business.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I used Evernote’s for everyday use and for work notes as well
author: Sara B.
pros: Liked how I could transfer notes from my phone to my laptop!
cons: I don’t think I had any negative experienced
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Zane S.
pros: Easy to use. Allows me to record and process lists effectively.
cons: Would be nice to figure out a way to send my notes on true context and collect notes from employees automatically.
